name: D3.js

performance.notes: SVG manipulation; performance scales with number of elements; canvas mode for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: The most powerful data visualization library for the web. Low-level but maximally flexible. Data joins for binding data to DOM elements. Scales for coordinate/color mapping. Layouts for force, tree, pack, partition. Geographic projections. Transitions and animations. Observable Plot (high-level wrapper) simplifies common charts. D3 enables any visualization imaginable. Industry standard for custom data journalism.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — data joins and general update pattern take time to master
Verbose for simple charts (use Vega-Lite/Observable Plot for standard charts)
v5→v6→v7 breaking changes in module structure
Performance degrades with thousands of SVG elements (use canvas mode)
best_when: Custom, bespoke visualizations not possible with chart libraries
Data journalism and storytelling with unique visual encoding
avoid_when: Standard charts (bar, line, scatter) — use Plotly/Recharts/Observable Plot
summary: D3.js is the most powerful and flexible JavaScript data visualization library. Low-level DOM manipulation with data joins, scales, and layouts. Enables any visualization imaginable. Industry standard for custom data journalism, interactive maps, and network visualizations. Steep learning curve — use Observable Plot or Vega-Lite for standard charts and reserve D3 for unique custom visualizations.
